sections.paragraph:
to limp, to become lame, prop. to incline to one side, Arab. ظع and ظاع id. (perhaps denom. from צֵלָע), Gen 32:32. Part. f. חַצֹּלֵעָה coll. limping flocks, i.e. flocks wearied with the heat and with journeying; used figuratively of the Israelites, Mic 4:6, Mic 4:7 Zep 3:19.

[Derivatives, the following words.]
